title: Champereia longistaminea
taxonomy: Champereia longistaminea is a member of the family Celastraceae. It belongs to the order Celastrales. The plant is further classified as belonging to the genus Champereia. Champereia longistaminea is native to Southeast Asia. The species is often found in tropical regions.
uses: Champereia longistaminea is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Research on this plant suggests that it may have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities. The leaves of Champereia longistaminea have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever and digestive problems. However, more scientific studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use. Further research is also required to unlock the full potential of this plant as a medicinal resource.

parts: Champereia longistaminea is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. preparations: Champereia longistaminea is primarily used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the creation of herbal preparations. intro: Champereia longistaminea is a plant species of the family Celastraceae, commonly known as a tropical vine or shrub native to Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Champereia longistaminea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of a tincture or extract, to take advantage of its purported health benefits.
